name: BESS — Battery Energy Storage System design & safety
standard: NFPA 855:2023 (Installation of Stationary Energy Storage Systems) + IEC 62619:2022 (Safety requirements — secondary lithium cells & batteries — industrial application) + IEC 62620:2014 (Performance requirements — lithium cells & batteries) + UL 9540:2023 + UL 9540A:2024 (Test method — fire propagation) + IEEE 2030.2 / IEEE 2030.3 (BESS interconnection) + NERC PRC-024 (US frequency / voltage ride-through)
description: Battery Energy Storage System rule pack. BESS is increasingly integrated into utility-scale renewables EPC scope (PV+BESS hybrid, standalone grid-services, ancillary frequency response). Validates: cell + system safety qualification (IEC 62619 / UL 9540 / UL 9540A unit-level fire propagation test), site layout per NFPA 855 (3 m separation between enclosures default, deflagration ventilation, exhaust ventilation, gas detection — H2 + CO + VOC, explosion control, fire access on at least 2 sides for utility-scale, hazard mitigation analysis HMA per NFPA 855 §4.1.4), thermal-runaway risk ALARP (As Low As Reasonably Practicable) assessment with emergency response plan, NFPA 855 deflagration testing UL 9540A 4-level (cell / module / unit / installation), augmentation strategy over 20-year design life (typical replacement at 65 – 70 % SoH; 1.5 – 2x oversizing or staged top-up), round-trip efficiency target ≥ 85 % AC-AC, depth-of-discharge management, cycle life vs warranty (typical 6 000 cycles @ 80 % DoD for LFP).
